payMixPhilosophy.note: Today most roles run 75-80% base / 20-25% variable. Goal is to gradually shift more compensation into variable pay (70:30) as team confidence in the plan grows and the business can support it.

gm.name: General Manager (CM-driven + NP kicker)
gm.description: For Hayley. $1M no-commission floor; $10k maintenance hit at $1,100,000 (round-down from LTM CM ~$1,111k); then escalating growth bands. Dashboard shows LTM CM live from companyPL. NP incremental bonuses are more lucrative with bands extending to $1.3M. Band output is super-INCLUSIVE. Quarterly 75/25 payout (being reviewed — may switch to quarterly TU like Sales team).

gp_bands_senior.name: Senior Sales Manager (GP Incremental Bands)
gp_bands_senior.description: For Senior Sales (Dane). Same band structure as the Sales Rep plan — seniority reflected in the $92.5k base salary rather than in the commission bands. Band output is super-INCLUSIVE. 1% production commission on Event GP (also super-inclusive). Quarterly true-up with running balance carry-forward.

gp_bands.name: Sales Rep (GP Incremental Bands)
gp_bands.description: For Sales Reps (Daniel, Nic). Five-band structure: true no-commission floor at $100k, soft start from $100-200k, ramps through $200-300k so $300k matches old 3.5% flat, accelerates past $400k. Band output is super-INCLUSIVE. 1% production commission on Event GP (also super-inclusive). Quarterly true-ups net against a running balance that carries forward (no clawback from base).

hybrid_gp.name: Sales-Producer Hybrid (GP Sales + Production)
hybrid_gp.description: For Evie & Evie 2.0. Five-band monotonic structure with $50k no-commission floor (half the Sales Rep floor). Match at $150k (old 3.5%+super ~$5,880), clear win at $200k (~$12,000 vs old+super $7,840 = +$4,160), and rates continue climbing above $200k at 13% — 2 points under Daniel's 15% top so Evie's stretch stays meaningful without out-paying Sales Rep stretch per dollar. 1% production commission on Event GP drives the bulk of Evie's earnings. Band output is super-INCLUSIVE.

hybrid_rev.name: Talent (Performer-Revenue Hybrid)
hybrid_rev.description: For Michael (Performer/Systems Manager). Variable comp on revenue bands. $70k floor (pay-for-yourself), then $30k increments up to $190k+. Band rates scaled × 1.12 in v16 so band output is super-INCLUSIVE (same commission amounts as before, just stated inclusive of super).

hybrid_rev_senior.name: Talent Senior (Performer-Revenue Hybrid)
hybrid_rev_senior.description: For Tom (Performer/Talent Manager). Higher-base version of the Talent plan with thresholds shifted to reflect his senior base salary of $91.35k. Target: $220k revenue → ~$100k base ex-super equivalent. Rates scaled × 1.12 in v16 to make band output super-INCLUSIVE while preserving commission amounts.

kpiScorecard.overallNote: DRAFT v2 — rebuilt from the GM Position Description (pp. 14–21 of the Australian Staff Employment Agreement). Anchored on the eight 'What Success Looks Like in Year One' markers plus Operational Leadership, Team Leadership & HR, Financial Management, Interim HOE Coverage, and behavioural standards.
kpiScorecard.sourceDoc: Australian Staff Employment Agreement.pdf — General Manager PD

categories.title: Operational Leadership & Team Cadence
criteria: Team operating to a clear, consistent meeting rhythm (regular 1:1s, performance check-ins, all-team cadence run effectively with your support).
Senior leadership team meeting cadence held — regular 1:1s with direct reports plus a monthly performance review with the Director.
Primary escalation point for operational issues — coaches direct reports through resolution rather than solving everything directly.
Proactively identifies operational friction and gaps — escalates improvement briefs to the Director with clear context.
Adopts and drives adherence to new systems and processes as they are implemented.

categories.title: Team Performance & Direct Reports
criteria: Every direct report has documented KPIs and has received at least one formal mid-year review with clear, honest feedback.
Onboarding is no longer improvised — at least one new team member has been brought on through a structured process you've built or refined.
Direct reports experience you as a GM, not a former peer — the leadership shift has been made and held with consistency.
Manages separations and performance improvement processes in collaboration with the Director.
Mentors direct reports through conflict resolution; holds the team accountable to Company-Wide Behaviours (above B average).

categories.title: Financial Execution
criteria: Financial performance tracked weekly — leading and lagging metrics reviewed, variances understood; brings the Director well-reasoned analysis rather than raw numbers.
Executes against annual revenue, GP, and cost targets set in partnership with the Director.
Monitors weekly/monthly leading and lagging metrics (quotes, confirmations, revenue, conversion rates, average value, call volumes) and acts on variances.
Oversees wage budgets and assesses cost efficiency regularly via Xero cash-basis P&L.
Ensures the business operates within break-even parameters and flags financial risk early.

categories.title: Interim HOE Coverage & Talent/Account Oversight
criteria: HOE vacancy hasn't created dropped balls — venue relationships, talent oversight, and account management are being covered with clear interim ownership.
Web project backlog is prioritised and moving — clear weekly mandate to the dev team; web decisions no longer bottleneck on the Director.
Brand & quality oversight: client interactions and event deliverables remain consistent with brand identity in tone, quality, and presentation.
Working rhythm with the Director gives genuine autonomy while keeping strategic alignment tight.

categories.title: Behavioural & Conduct Floor
criteria: Adheres to Company-Wide Behaviours and Individual Responsibilities; maintains an above-B weighted average across both.
Owns company culture, values, and the behavioural framework (Company-Wide Behaviours).
Continuously improves employee retention, satisfaction, and productivity.
Works diligently and amicably with all staff, clients, and suppliers.

kpiScorecard.overallNote: DRAFT — Senior Sales KPIs from employment agreement. NOTE: under review for FY26.

categories.title: Sales Performance
criteria: Gross Profit Sold > $375,000 in the calendar year
Bookings Quoted > 1,000 across the calendar year
Monthly conversion rate ≥ 35% across a 365-day period
Average GP of Sales > $800 per transaction every month when converting new business

categories.title: Behavioural & Conduct
criteria: Achieve a B or higher weighted average across Company-wide and Role-specific behavioural reviews
Work sufficiently diligently and amicably towards staff and clients of MEC in all work undertakings

kpiScorecard.overallNote: DRAFT — Sales & Talent Manager KPIs. Min performances/revenue de-emphasised; sourcing + content prioritised.

categories.title: Talent Sourcing & Onboarding
criteria: Source and on-board minimum 30 new entertainers per calendar year across multiple states
Melbourne replacement-of-turnover floor: 5 DJs, 2 DJ-Sax, 2 Complete Soloists

categories.title: Content & Photo Shoots
criteria: 25 in-event organised Content Ninja video shoots across the calendar year
Schedule and roll out 2 separate photo shoots across the calendar year (with Production Coordinator)
Capture content for 40+ artists annually across these shoots

categories.title: Sales Contribution
criteria: Personal Gross Profit Sold above the agreed annual target (subject to change with sales involvement)
Maintain Average GP per booking > $800 when converting new business

categories.title: Behavioural & Conduct
criteria: Achieve a B or higher weighted average across Company-wide and Role-specific behavioural reviews
Work sufficiently diligently and amicably towards staff and clients of MEC in all work undertakings

kpiScorecard.overallNote: Slider value 0–100% per category × weight. Weighted sum maps linearly: 0%→0.7×, 50%→1.0×, 100%→1.3× multiplier on commission.

categories.title: Performance Reliability & Quality
criteria: 100% fulfilment rate for all personally booked performances (no late arrivals, no poor cancellations)
Minimum 35 performances during contract period (pro-rated from annual 70 target)
20% 5-star review rate or higher (35 × 20% = 7 reviews)
Increase gross profit per performance by 25% vs previous year personal average

categories.title: Initiative & Proactivity
criteria: Demonstrate proactive mindset — identify improvements without prompting
Propose at least 2 improvements to systems/workflows/tools by Month 3
Independently research and trial 1 new tool, present findings to management
Show evidence of troubleshooting/solving issues without escalation, at least once per fortnight

categories.title: Systems Development & Delivery
criteria: Successfully scope, build, and implement at least 1 major internal system/tool (e.g. Talent Availability Checker, Client Online Portal, Custom Quote Generator)
Deliver 3–5 smaller automation/system improvements within Zoho/PandaDoc/MEC platforms (≥5 hours/month admin saved)
Detailed documentation (technical notes, SOPs, UAT reports) for every system built/updated

categories.title: Training & Documentation
criteria: Refactor and update internal DJ training modules — record new videos where necessary
Create minimum 5 new SOP videos covering backend systems or performance tech, stored in internal library
Maintain training content version-controlled and up-to-date by end of contract

categories.title: Interpersonal Impact & Team Support
criteria: Resolve 90% of assigned IT support requests within agreed timeframes (24–48h non-urgent, same-day urgent)
Deliver at least 4 DJ mentoring/shadowing sessions for new performers, with positive feedback
Active participation in weekly leadership/project meetings — clear progress updates
Strong, transparent communication — weekly progress emails on current projects
Attend 100% of scheduled team meetings unless on leave/performing
Contribute positively to team culture (peak-period support, helpful knowledge sharing)
Gather feedback on new projects from at least 3 team members by Month 5

categories.title: Company-wide & Behavioural Expectations
criteria: Achieve a B or higher weighted average across Company-wide and Role-specific behavioural reviews
Meet 90% of project deadlines as agreed in project timelines
Maintain a collaborative, solutions-focused approach with all staff, clients, and suppliers
Work sufficiently diligently and amicably towards staff and clients of MEC in all undertakings

companyPL.label: Company P&L (Cash Basis)
companyPL.subLabel: Quarterly company financials. Feeds the company-wide view and can gate variable comp payouts (e.g., 'comm only pays if company NP > X'). Fiscal year starts July.

xeroIntegration.note: TODO: create an integration that pulls in directly from Xero. In the meantime, use CSV upload or manual entry.

financialPlanning.zohoNote: Zoho GP sold is slightly lower than Xero cash-basis GP due to internal buffers. Use Zoho figures for commission calc; Xero ratios used here for company P&L projection.
